mnemonics: PHAT: Parameters (null and alternative), Hypothesis test, Alpha level, Test statistic
keyPoints: A test of significance assesses the evidence provided by data against a null hypothesis (H0) and in favor of an alternative hypothesis (Ha).
The p-value is the probability, assuming H0 is true, that the test statistic would take a value as extreme or more extreme than that actually observed.
A result is statistically significant if the p-value is less than or equal to the chosen significance level (α), suggesting strong evidence against H0.
commonMistakes: Concluding that a small p-value proves the alternative hypothesis is true; it only indicates evidence against the null.
Assuming that a non-significant result (large p-value) proves the null hypothesis is true; it only suggests a lack of strong evidence against it.
Confusing statistical significance with practical importance; a result can be statistically significant but have a trivial real-world effect.
